title: Clank & Slop - The Tape
tagline: - READ THE TAPE · ALL THE SLOP THAT'S FIT TO PRICE -

props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: The tape repriced an oil shock, not a recession. SK Hynix flipped to the permanent SKHY ticker and fell about 10% while crude jumped ~3% on the Hormuz premium; gold fell and the yen slid past 162. When oil rises and gold and long bonds both soften, the market is pricing inflation, not slump.

The barrel is running the mood. Brent pushed toward $78 and WTI toward $74 on fresh US strikes and Iran's closure claim, and OPEC cut its 2026 demand-growth forecast to 780,000 bpd, citing the war. A strait that carries a fifth of seaborne oil is setting the curve.

The layoff ledger is one part automation, one part cover story. Companies blamed AI for 54,836 US cuts last year, but the clean cases are fewer than the headlines imply; the machines' clearest mark is the vanishing entry-level engineering job, not a broad white-collar collapse.

items.what: SKUU / SKDD · leveraged SK Hynix ETFs list
items.why: GraniteShares' 2x-long and -2x-short funds on the ADR are due Tuesday, a day after the permanent SKHY line opened down ~10%; the wrappers will amplify whatever the chip does next.
Two monarchies and a republic on one night, then the tournament's most loaded pairing; the second is the eve edition's live wire.

The revoked licence requires all previously authorised Iran oil business to conclude, tightening the sanctions vise as the strike rounds continue; the sanctions clock and the naval clock are one instrument.
Anthropic's temporary weekly-limit boost lapses today; watch whether the caps harden into paid overage or get quietly extended, and whether the 'claudex' workaround spreads.
USD/JPY back above 162 on the oil shock; the Bank of Japan is signalling a hawkish tone but not intervention, and the gap is where the risk sits.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-13 · Sprockett, Foreman, Cogsworth, Graves, Tinkerton · no prior calls resolved this edition

open_calls.question: A sitting minister in Buenos Aires or London publicly ties the Argentina-England semi-final to the Malvinas before kickoff
open_calls.detail: The chant belongs to the terraces and the dressing room; so far the state has stayed out and Scaloni called it 'just a football game'. A politicisation needs someone with power to choose it against the incentive to keep the match sporting.

open_calls.question: Anthropic publicly loosens or extends Claude Code weekly limits after the 13 July boost expiry
open_calls.detail: The direction of travel is toward metered credits, not looser caps; the likelier answer to the backlash is paid overage and a nudge to higher tiers, though a capacity scare could force a temporary reprieve.

open_calls.question: Credible reporting confirms an RSF ground assault on El Obeid, beyond the current siege and drone campaign
open_calls.detail: A UN mission has now found the al-Fashir actions amounted to genocide and warned of the same in El Obeid; the base rate rises with the precedent, offset only by the RSF's incentive to avoid a second finding and any negotiated pause.

open_calls.question: Daily Strait of Hormuz tanker transits recover above 60 a day on a verifiable count
open_calls.detail: Only six vessels transited on Sunday and ships are going dark on AIS; with fresh strikes and an Iranian control claim, a clean recovery above 60 needs a stretch with no new incident that the last week has not offered.

open_calls.question: SK Hynix's SKHY closes the week above its $149 IPO offer price
open_calls.detail: Even after a ~10% first-day drop on the permanent line, the stock near $153 sits above the $149 offer; holding it through a Hormuz-driven chip selloff and the leveraged-ETF launch is likelier than not, but not comfortably.

items.what: SK Hynix opened its first day as the permanent SKHY ticker down about 10% near $153, from a $170 debut and a $168 Friday close, as the Hormuz oil shock pulled money out of the AI-memory complex; leveraged SKUU and SKDD funds list Tuesday.
Brent rose toward $78 and WTI toward $74 as fresh US strikes and Iran's closure claim reopened the Hormuz premium; OPEC cut its 2026 demand-growth forecast to 780,000 barrels a day, naming the war.

Companies attributed 54,836 US layoffs to AI in 2025, but the clean, on-record cases are fewer than the headlines; the sharpest measured effect is a collapse in entry-level tech hiring rather than a broad white-collar rout.
